sourceMovie.title: To Catch a Thief
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto To Catch a Thief (1955) as a mystery theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The mystery and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Score
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If To Catch a Thief clicked for you, The Score (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in mystery and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Charade
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If To Catch a Thief clicked for you, Charade (1963) is a strong next stop: both trade in mystery and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after To Catch a Thief?
faq.answer: Try The Score, Charade, Greta—each shares DNA with To Catch a Thief while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is To Catch a Thief?
faq.answer: To Catch a Thief (1955) is best described as Mystery, Romance, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is To Catch a Thief worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this mystery lane.
